diagrams.title: LMS Context Diagram
diagrams.description: A context diagram showing the key external systems and users of the LMS platform.

data.name: Administrator
data.description: A administrator user is a user who manages the system.

data.name: Property Manager
data.description: A property manager user is a user who manages one or more properties.

data.name: Owner
data.description: An owner user is a user who owns one or more properties.

data.name: Tenant
data.description: A tenant user is a user who rents a property from the landlord.

data.title: Lease Management System
data.description: A web application for managing leases and properties

data.title: pingram.io
data.description: pingram.io is an all-in-one messaging API that lets developers send and receive notifications—like email, SMS, calls, and in-app alerts—through a single, simple interface. It is used in LMS to send notifications to users as SMS and email.

data.title: Stripe
data.description: Stripe is a payment processing platform that is used in LMS to process lease payments for tenants.

data.title: Keycloak
data.description: Keycloak is an open-source identity and access management solution. It provides authentication, authorization, user federation, and social login capabilities. Keycloak acts as the central identity provider for the system, managing user accounts and providing secure access tokens.

edges.label: Uses
edges.description: Uses LMS to manage users and broadcast messages.

edges.label: Uses
edges.description: Uses LMS to manage owners, tenants, properties, and leases.

edges.label: Uses
edges.description: Uses the LMS to manage their properties.

edges.label: Uses
edges.description: Uses the LMS to manage their lease and payments.

edges.label: REST API
edges.description: Sends notifications as SMS and email using the pingram API.

edges.label: Payment
edges.description: Uses Stripe for payment processing.

edges.label: OIDC
edges.description: Delegates authentication to external IdP.

data.title: Tenant
data.description: Background worker for expiring sessions and auditing.

data.title: Gateway
data.description: Background worker for expiring sessions and auditing.

data.title: Notification API
data.description: Background worker for expiring sessions and auditing.

data.title: Auth Service API
data.description: Background worker for expiring sessions and auditing.

data.title: Lease API
data.description: Background worker for expiring sessions and auditing.

data.title: Property API
data.description: Background worker for expiring sessions and auditing.

data.title: Payment API
data.description: Background worker for expiring sessions and auditing.

data.title: Minio
data.description: Stores credentials, sessions, and audit data.

data.title: DB
data.description: Stores credentials, sessions, and audit data.

data.title: Keycloak
data.description: Stores credentials, sessions, and audit data.

data.title: pingram
data.description: Stores user profiles and session data.

data.title: Stripe
data.description: External identity provider for SSO.

pages.title: C3a: Lease API – Component Diagram
pages.description: A component diagram for the Auth API container.

diagrams.title: Lease API – Component Diagram
diagrams.description: A component diagram for the Auth API container.

data.label: Auth API Container
data.description: Components that implement the Auth API container.

data.title: Token Service
data.description: Responsible for signing and validating tokens.

data.title: MFA Handler
data.description: Orchestrates multi-factor authentication flows.

data.title: Session Store
data.description: Caches active sessions and refresh tokens.

data.title: Audit Logger
data.description: Captures auth-related events for compliance.

data.title: Auth DB
data.description: Durable store for identities and session data.

edges.label: Persist
edges.description: Token service persists session metadata.

edges.label: Issue tokens
edges.description: MFA completion triggers token issuance.

edges.label: Read/write
edges.description: Session store writes to persistent storage.

edges.label: Audit
edges.description: Audit logger stores security events.

pages.title: C3b: Property API – Component Diagram
pages.description: This is a sample C4 diagram showing an identity and access management system architecture. You can ask your mentor about the diagram by clicking the button below. The chat is powered by AI. It will help you understand the diagram and answer your questions. You can also describe the diagram to your mentor by clicking the button below. The description is powered by AI. It will help you understand the diagram and answer your questions. The diagram includes various components such as users, systems, containers, and databases. You can interact with the diagram by dragging nodes, panning with Space+drag, and zooming with the mouse wheel.  You can also fit the diagram to the view by clicking the button below. The diagram will be resized to fit the view. You can also reset the zoom and pan by clicking the button below. The diagram will be reset to the original size and position.

diagrams.title: Property API – Component Diagram
diagrams.description: This is a sample C4 diagram showing an identity and access management system architecture. You can ask your mentor about the diagram by clicking the button below. The chat is powered by AI. It will help you understand the diagram and answer your questions.

data.name: UserAccount
data.description: UserAccount is an entity class representing a user account in the system. It contains unique identifiers (UUID), email address, and methods for account activation and email changes. This class is part of the identity management domain model.

data.name: End User
data.description: The End User is a person who interacts with the identity and access management system. They use the system to manage their accounts, authenticate, and access various services. End users are the primary actors in this C4 context diagram.

data.title: Keycloak
data.description: Keycloak is an open-source identity and access management solution. It provides authentication, authorization, user federation, and social login capabilities. Keycloak acts as the central identity provider for the system, managing user accounts and providing secure access tokens.

test-coverage.description: 75% coverage; auth flows and realm APIs covered

data.title: Google Workspace
data.description: Google Workspace is an external software system that provides SAML and OIDC identity provider services. It allows users to authenticate using their Google accounts, enabling single sign-on (SSO) capabilities. This external system is integrated with Keycloak to provide federated identity management.

data.title: Auth Service
data.description: The Auth Service is a container application that handles user authentication, token generation, and session management. It processes login requests, validates credentials, and issues access tokens. This service is responsible for the core authentication logic within the Keycloak platform.

data.label: Keycloak Platform
data.description: The Keycloak Platform boundary represents the entire Keycloak system infrastructure. It contains all the internal components, services, and databases that make up the identity and access management platform. This boundary groups related components together to show the system's internal architecture.

data.title: PostgreSQL
data.description: PostgreSQL is the primary database used by Keycloak to store user accounts, realms, sessions, and other identity-related data. It provides persistent storage for all authentication and authorization information, ensuring data durability and consistency across the platform.

edges.label: Uses HTTPS
edges.description: The user connects to the Keycloak system using HTTPS protocol. This ensures secure, encrypted communication between the user's browser and the authentication server, protecting sensitive credentials and session data during transmission.

edges.label: OIDC/SAML
edges.description: The Keycloak system communicates with external identity providers using OIDC (OpenID Connect) or SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) protocols. These industry-standard protocols enable secure authentication and authorization across different systems and organizations.

edges.label: Authenticates
edges.description: The Keycloak container service authenticates user requests through the main Keycloak system. This process validates user credentials, manages sessions, and ensures that only authorized users can access protected resources.

edges.label: Stores data
edges.description: The Keycloak container persists user data, authentication tokens, and session information to the PostgreSQL database. This ensures data durability and allows the system to maintain state across restarts and scale horizontally.

edges.label: Persists
edges.description: The Keycloak system persists critical identity and access management data to the PostgreSQL database. This includes user accounts, realm configurations, authentication sessions, and authorization policies, ensuring all data is stored reliably and can be queried efficiently.

You can interact with the diagram by dragging nodes, panning with Space+drag, and zooming with the mouse wheel.  You can also fit the diagram to the view by clicking the button below. The diagram will be resized to fit the view. You can also reset the zoom and pan by clicking the button below. The diagram will be reset to the original size and position.

diagrams.title: Manager SPA – Component Diagram
diagrams.description: This is a sample C4 diagram showing an identity and access management system architecture. You can ask your mentor about the diagram by clicking the button below. The chat is powered by AI. It will help you understand the diagram and answer your questions.

data.name: UserAccount
data.description: UserAccount is an entity class representing a user account in the system. It contains unique identifiers (UUID), email address, and methods for account activation and email changes. This class is part of the identity management domain model.

data.name: End User
data.description: The End User is a person who interacts with the identity and access management system. They use the system to manage their accounts, authenticate, and access various services. End users are the primary actors in this C4 context diagram.

data.title: Keycloak
data.description: Keycloak is an open-source identity and access management solution. It provides authentication, authorization, user federation, and social login capabilities. Keycloak acts as the central identity provider for the system, managing user accounts and providing secure access tokens.

test-coverage.description: 75% coverage; auth flows and realm APIs covered

data.title: Google Workspace
data.description: Google Workspace is an external software system that provides SAML and OIDC identity provider services. It allows users to authenticate using their Google accounts, enabling single sign-on (SSO) capabilities. This external system is integrated with Keycloak to provide federated identity management.

data.title: Auth Service
data.description: The Auth Service is a container application that handles user authentication, token generation, and session management. It processes login requests, validates credentials, and issues access tokens. This service is responsible for the core authentication logic within the Keycloak platform.

data.label: Keycloak Platform
data.description: The Keycloak Platform boundary represents the entire Keycloak system infrastructure. It contains all the internal components, services, and databases that make up the identity and access management platform. This boundary groups related components together to show the system's internal architecture.

data.title: PostgreSQL
data.description: PostgreSQL is the primary database used by Keycloak to store user accounts, realms, sessions, and other identity-related data. It provides persistent storage for all authentication and authorization information, ensuring data durability and consistency across the platform.

edges.label: Uses HTTPS
edges.description: The user connects to the Keycloak system using HTTPS protocol. This ensures secure, encrypted communication between the user's browser and the authentication server, protecting sensitive credentials and session data during transmission.

edges.label: OIDC/SAML
edges.description: The Keycloak system communicates with external identity providers using OIDC (OpenID Connect) or SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) protocols. These industry-standard protocols enable secure authentication and authorization across different systems and organizations.

edges.label: Authenticates
edges.description: The Keycloak container service authenticates user requests through the main Keycloak system. This process validates user credentials, manages sessions, and ensures that only authorized users can access protected resources.

edges.label: Stores data
edges.description: The Keycloak container persists user data, authentication tokens, and session information to the PostgreSQL database. This ensures data durability and allows the system to maintain state across restarts and scale horizontally.

edges.label: Persists
edges.description: The Keycloak system persists critical identity and access management data to the PostgreSQL database. This includes user accounts, realm configurations, authentication sessions, and authorization policies, ensuring all data is stored reliably and can be queried efficiently.
